action.hypothesis: Switching the current strongest K=16 backbone from signum to Adam may improve low-K optimization under the fixed EF=16 regime, because routed soft-codebook residual paths likely need smoother magnitude-aware updates than sign-based steps.
action.summary: Hadamard preprocessing failed badly at K=16, so preprocessing skepticism should increase. The codebase does not expose an alternative loss yet, and the only supported under-explored training recipe dimension available without editing code is optimizer choice. I am testing `OPTIMIZER=adam` on the current K=16 frontier family to keep the hypothesis single-variable while targeting the highest-value smaller-K lane.
action.self_review: This follows the operator priority shift toward optimizer exploration and avoids more architecture churn. The main risk is that EF=16 regime drift may dominate the signal, but testing Adam on the incumbent K=16 family is still the cleanest way to measure whether optimization, not architecture, is now the limiter.
notes_to_memory: Round 123 shifts from failed preprocessing to optimizer exploration because `USE_HADAMARD=1` strongly degraded K=16 performance.
The current codebase exposes only `signum` and `adam` as optimizer choices; no alternative configurable loss is available without editing `sparsify/`.
Target the incumbent K=16 routed two-stage soft-codebook family so the result isolates optimizer effects in the highest-value small-K lane under the new fixed EF=16 policy.
next_hypotheses: If Adam improves K=16, propagate Adam to K=32 on the strongest matching backbone to test whether the benefit generalizes beyond the smallest frontier-relevant slot.
If Adam is neutral or worse on this routed family, try Adam on `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at K=32 rather than continuing on routing-heavy variants.
If optimizer changes continue to fail under EF=16, run one EF=16 signum control on a second strong existing family to determine whether the fixed-EF regime itself is the dominant regression source.

result.description: Hadamard preprocessing failed badly at K=16, so preprocessing skepticism should increase. I am testing `OPTIMIZER=adam` on the current K=16 frontier family to keep the hypothesis single-variable while targeting the highest-value smaller-K lane.

The main risk is that EF=16 regime drift may dominate the signal, but testing Adam on the incumbent K=16 family is still the cleanest way to measure whether optimization, not architecture, is now the limiter.
